div.p: Execution Time vs Block Size — Finding the Sweet Spot

Key Insight: The optimal block size is architecture-dependent: • L1 size determines maximum block • Element type affects fit (float vs double) • Prefetcher behavior influences overhead Auto-tuning finds the empirical optimum without knowing cache parameters.

Why U-shaped? • Too Small (B=8-16): High loop
overhead, few operations per block • Optimal (B=32-64): Block fits L1,
good reuse, low overhead • Too Large (B=128+): Block spills
to L2/L3, cache pollution
